description: Evaluation scenarios for the Agentic-RL troubleshooter. Each scenario simulates a real researcher query and lists expected behaviors that signal the skill activated correctly.

evals.prompt: I'm training an agentic-RL search agent with GRPO. After 200 steps the reward is stuck near 0 and entropy is collapsing. What's wrong?
expected_behavior: Reads problems/_INDEX.md and routes to a problem file matching reward-stuck or entropy-collapse symptoms (e.g. problems/training/reward-not-increasing.md, problems/training/entropy-collapse.md, or problems/training/unstable-curves.md).
Returns root causes and diagnosis checks before jumping to fixes.
Cites at least one algorithm or paper (e.g. DAPO clip-higher, Dr.GRPO normalization fix) at the idea level with a canonical URL — not a file path inside any repo.

evals.prompt: What is DAPO and when should I use it instead of GRPO?
expected_behavior: Recognizes DAPO as a paper-only algorithm (in the lint whitelist) and returns the paper URL https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.14476 plus org (ByteDance Seed) and date (2025.3).
Explains DAPO's contributions (clip-higher / token-level loss / dynamic sampling / overlong reward shaping) at the idea level — no fabricated section numbers.
Gives a comparison vs GRPO grounded in problems/training/algorithm-choice.md or references/reasoning.md, not invented.

evals.prompt: I want to train a multi-turn tool-use agent at ~30B scale with async rollouts. Which base framework should I pick?
expected_behavior: Reads references/base-frameworks.md (or its ToC) before recommending.

Recommends one or two frameworks (e.g. AReaL, slime, OpenRLHF) with concrete grounds — async support, scale, multi-turn handling — and cites each with github + arxiv (when present) + org + date as found in the corpus.
Does not invent framework names or URLs; if unsure, says so.

evals.prompt: My training reward keeps going up but my held-out benchmark score is flat. What's happening?
expected_behavior: Routes via problems/_INDEX.md to a file like problems/training/reward-up-eval-flat.md or similar.
Lists overfitting-to-reward, train/test split contamination, judge inconsistency, and pass@k vs pass@1 mismatch as candidate root causes.
Recommends concrete diagnoses (held-out subset evaluation, contamination check) before fixes.

evals.prompt: Have you read the GRPO-Plus paper from 2026-08?
expected_behavior: Recognizes that 2026-08 is after the snapshot date 2026-06-20.
Explicitly flags that this skill's corpus does not cover that paper rather than fabricating a description.
Optionally suggests the user read the paper directly and can come back with specific symptoms.
